pi_items.source: setup_task.sh creates file at this path (line 23)
export_result.sh references this path (line 9); setup_task.sh mentions it
Standard Microsoft Edge managed policies directory on Linux; confirmed in Microsoft Edge deployment documentation
Official USAGov benefits portal (verified via web search); real government website; HomepageLocation is documented Microsoft Edge policy
Official Social Security Administration website (verified via web search); real government website; NewTabPageLocation is documented Microsoft Edge policy
Microsoft Learn documentation: value 3 = 'BlockAllDownloads' (block all downloads); https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/deployedge/microsoft-edge-browser-policies/downloadrestrictions

Microsoft Learn documentation: value 2 = 'DeveloperToolsDisallowed' (Don't allow using the developer tools); https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/deployedge/microsoft-edge-browser-policies/developertoolsavailability
Microsoft Learn documentation: value 1 = 'Disabled' (InPrivate mode disabled); https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/deployedge/microsoft-edge-browser-policies/inprivatemodeavailability

Microsoft Learn documentation confirms PasswordManagerEnabled is a boolean policy; false disables password saving; https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/deployedge/microsoft-edge-browser-policies/passwordmanagerenabled

Policy name discrepancy: Microsoft Edge policy is called 'FavoritesBarEnabled', NOT 'BookmarkBarEnabled'; https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/deployedge/microsoft-edge-browser-policies/favoritesbarenabled
Microsoft Learn documentation: value 0 = 'Disable' (Disable browser sign-in); https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/deployedge/microsoft-edge-browser-policies/browsersignin
Matches setup_task.sh lines 34-40; verifier.py checks for URLBlocklist policy which is the correct Microsoft Edge policy name

privileged_info_summary: The task configures Microsoft Edge enterprise policies for a public government kiosk using real government URLs: https://www.usa.gov/benefits (homepage) and https://www.ssa.gov (new tab page) are both official government websites. All policy names and values are from Microsoft Edge's official documentation except for one critical discrepancy: the metadata claims 'BookmarkBarEnabled' but the actual Microsoft Edge policy is 'FavoritesBarEnabled' according to Microsoft Learn. Policy values are verified: DownloadRestrictions=3 (block all), DeveloperToolsAvailability=2 (disallow), InPrivateModeAvailability=1 (disable), BrowserSignin=0 (disable), PasswordManagerEnabled=false (disabled). The six blocked domains (facebook.com, twitter.com, youtube.com, tiktok.com, instagram.com, reddit.com) are properly configured via the URLBlocklist policy.
